assertions.title: CLI auth/operator flow and web approval surface use consistent Papierklammer naming

steps.action: Run `pnpm --dir "/Users/aischool/work/papierklammer_droid" papierklammer auth login --help` and then start an isolated real auth flow with `PAPIERKLAMMER_HOME=<evidence-home> pnpm --dir "/Users/aischool/work/papierklammer_droid" papierklammer auth login --api-base "http://127.0.0.1:3100"`.
steps.expected: The initiating CLI surface uses the renamed `papierklammer` command family and emits an approval URL for the real browser flow.
steps.observed: Help output showed `Usage: papierklammer auth login [options]`. The live auth flow printed `Board authentication required`, emitted a real approval URL, and preserved `papierklammer auth login` in the terminal transcript before completing successfully for user `local-board`.

steps.action: Open the emitted approval URL in the assigned browser session and inspect the pending approval page before approving it.
steps.expected: The browser approval surface should use the same renamed Papierklammer product/command family and must not say `Paperclip CLI` or show legacy link targets.
steps.observed: The page headline was `Approve Papierklammer CLI access`; body copy said `A local Papierklammer CLI process is requesting board access to this instance.`; the command field showed `papierklammer auth login`; no links were present and no `Paperclip`/`paperclipai` text was visible.

steps.action: Approve the real challenge in the browser and inspect the resulting success state plus the CLI transcript.
steps.expected: Approval success should keep the renamed wording and allow the waiting CLI auth flow to finish.
steps.observed: After clicking `Approve CLI access`, the browser showed `CLI access approved` and `The Papierklammer CLI can now finish authentication on the requesting machine.` with `Command: papierklammer auth login`; the waiting CLI process finished with `{"ok": true, "userId": "local-board"}`.

evidence.legacyTokenCheck: No visible `Paperclip` or `paperclipai` text/link targets were observed on the approval flow; the CLI and browser both used `Papierklammer`/`papierklammer auth login`.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: VAL-CROSS-002 passed. The terminal-side auth flow and the real browser approval page both used the renamed `Papierklammer` / `papierklammer auth login` wording, with no legacy `Paperclip` or `paperclipai` text observed.
